refsect1.title: Description
refsect1.para: The ne_strnonce function can be used to generate a nonce value. The function returns a nonce as a base64-encoded or ASCII hexadecimal string.

The lenhint parameter suggests the length in bytes of random data to generate before encoding. If zero, a default length is used. The actual length of the returned string will be longer due to Base64 encoding overhead.
The flags parameter is reserved for future use and must be zero.

When &neon; is built with SSL/TLS support, the nonce is generated using cryptographically secure random number generation from the underlying SSL library (OpenSSL or GnuTLS). Without SSL support, a fallback implementation is used which combines timing and process information; this fallback is not cryptographically secure and should not be relied upon for security-sensitive applications.

refsect1.title: Return value
refsect1.para: The return value is a malloc-allocated, NUL-terminated Base64-encoded ASCII string, or &null; if the nonce cannot be generated. The caller must free the returned string using ne_free.

refsect1.title: Examples
refsect1.para: Generate a nonce using default parameters:
Generate a nonce with a specific length hint:
refsect1.programlisting: char *nonce = ne_strnonce(32, 0); /* nonce will be base64 of 32 random bytes */

refsect1.title: History
refsect1.para: The ne_strnonce function is available in &neon; 0.37.0 and later.
